root_text.sn10.11:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn10.11:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro upāsako cīrāya bhikkhuniyā cīvaraṁ adāsi.
root_text.sn10.11:1.4: Atha kho cīrāya bhikkhuniyā abhippasanno yakkho rājagahe rathikāya rathikaṁ siṅghāṭakena siṅghāṭakaṁ upasaṅkamitvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:

translation_text.sn10.11:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn10.11:1.3: Now at that time a certain lay follower gave a robe to the nun Cīrā.
translation_text.sn10.11:1.4: Then a native spirit was so devoted to Cīrā that he went from street to street and from square to square, and on that occasion recited these verses:
translation_text.sn10.11:2.1: “O! He has made so much merit!
translation_text.sn10.11:2.2: That lay-follower is so very wise.
translation_text.sn10.11:2.3: He gave a robe to Cīrā,
translation_text.sn10.11:2.4: who is released from all yokes.”
